documentation.description:
| Purpose
|   Command_Line_Processor is an abstract state machine
| that allows the user to access a command line, which
| may contain file references which are include files,
| as a simple list of file names which can be accessed
| via an interator and a Get function.  The command line
| syntax is:
|
|      command input_file input_file ... input_file
|
| where any "input_file" may be prefixed by an "@"
| to make it an include file.
|
| Modifications
|   2/19/90  Rick Conn  Initial Design and Code
|   2/27/96  Rick Conn  Adapted to SCATKIT

| Purpose
| Return TRUE if no more file names are
| available.
|
| Exceptions (none)

| Purpose
| Return the name of the next file.  Advance the
| iterator.
|
| Exceptions
|   END_OF_FILE_LIST

| Purpose
| Return the number of file names on the command line
| counting both the regular file names and the file
| names in the include files.
|
| Exceptions (none)

| Purpose
| Reset the iterator.  It is not necessary to
| call this routine the first time the processor
| is used.
|
| Exceptions (none)
